name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَإِذْ نَجَّيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ يُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَآءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكُم بَلَآءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (٤٩)
```

```english
[Remember] when We delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan who inflicted a terrible torment on you, and slaughtered your sons and spared your women, and in that there was a great test from your Lord. (49)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ فَرَقْنَا بِكُمُ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰكُمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ (٥٠)
```

```english
And when We parted the sea with you, and We delivered you and drowned Pharaoh’s clan as you looked on. (50)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ وَٰعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰٓ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذْتُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَنتُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (٥١)
```

```english
And when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights, you took up the Calf [for worship] in his absence, and you were wrongdoers. (51)
```

```arabic
ثُمَّ عَفَوْنَا عَنكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٥٢)
```

```english
Then We excused you after that so that you might give thanks. (52)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْفُرْقَانَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (٥٣)
```

```english
And when We gave Moses the Book and the Criterion so that you might be guided. (53)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنفُسَكُم بِٱتِّخَاذِكُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ فَتُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ بَارِئِكُمْ فَٱقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ عِندَ بَارِئِكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٥٤)
```

```english
And [recall] when Moses said to his people, ‘O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves by taking up the Calf [for worship]. Now turn penitently to your Maker, and slay [the guilty among] your folks. That will be better for you with your Maker.’ Then He turned to you clemently. Indeed, He is the All-clement, the All-merciful. (54)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ نَرَى ٱللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ (٥٥)
```

```english
And when you said, ‘O Moses, we will not believe you until we see Allah visibly.’ Thereupon a thunderbolt seized you as you looked on. (55)
```

```arabic
ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٥٦)
```

```english
Then We raised you up after your death so that you might give thanks. (56)
```

```arabic
وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (٥٧)
```

```english
We shaded you with clouds, and sent down to you manna and quails [saying]: ‘Eat of the good things We have provided for you.’ And they did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves. (57)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ قُلْنَا ٱدْخُلُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةَ فَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ رَغَدًا وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُولُوا۟ حِطَّةٌ نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطَـٰيَـٰكُمْ ۚ وَسَنَزِيدُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٥٨)
```

```english
And when We said, ‘Enter this town, and eat thereof freely whencesoever you wish, and enter while prostrating at the gate, and say, ‘‘Relieve [us of the burden of our sins],’’ so that We may forgive your iniquities and We will soon enhance the virtuous.’ (58)
```

```arabic
فَبَدَّلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ قَوْلًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رِجْزًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (٥٩)
```

```english
But the wrongdoers changed the saying with other than what they were told. So We sent down on those who were wrongdoers a plague from the sky because of the transgressions they used to commit. (59)
```

```arabic
۞ وَإِذِ ٱسْتَسْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ ۖ فَٱنفَجَرَتْ مِنْهُ ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۖ كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (٦٠)
```

```english
And when Moses prayed for water for his people, We said, ‘Strike the rock with your staff.’ Thereat twelve fountains gushed forth from it; every tribe came to know its drinking-place. ‘Eat and drink of Allah’s provision, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’ (60)
```

```arabic
وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نَّصْبِرَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامٍ وَٰحِدٍ فَٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُخْرِجْ لَنَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مِنۢ بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّآئِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا ۖ قَالَ أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ ٱلَّذِى هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِٱلَّذِى هُوَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِصْرًا فَإِنَّ لَكُم مَّا سَأَلْتُمْ ۗ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ وَٱلْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ (٦١)
```

```english
And when you said, ‘O Moses, ‘We will not put up with one kind of food. So invoke your Lord for us, so that He may bring forth for us of that which the earth grows—its greens and cucumbers, its garlic, lentils, and onions.’ He said, ‘Do you seek to replace what is superior with that which is inferior? Go down to any town and you will indeed get what you ask for!’ So they were struck with abasement and poverty, and they earned Allah’s wrath. That, because they would deny the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly. That, because they would disobey and commit transgressions. (61)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** *and let your women live* (ويستحيون نساءكم), meaning they left them alive for service rather than killing them as they did the sons. Thus, *istihya’* (الإستحياء) is the seeking of life (الحيوة). It is also possible that the meaning is that they committed acts of indecency that caused the loss of their modesty (حياء). The meaning of *yasumunakum* (يسومونكم) is that they inflicted or imposed upon you.

**His saying, the Exalted:** *And when We parted the sea for you* (إذ فرقنا بكم البحر). Parting (الفرق) is the opposite of joining, similar to separation (الفصل) and connection (الوصل). Parting in the sea refers to splitting it. The letter *ba* (ب) denotes causality (السببية) or accompaniment (الملابسة); that is, "We parted the sea because of your deliverance" or "due to your accompaniment in entering the sea."

**His saying, the Exalted:** *And when We appointed for Moses forty nights* (واعدنا موسى اربعين ليلة). The Exalted narrated this story in Surah al-A‘raf by His saying: **وَوَاعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰ ثَلَاثِينَ لَيْلَةً وَأَتْمَمْنَاهَا بِعَشْرٍ فَتَمَّ مِيقَاتُ رَبِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً** — *We appointed for Moses thirty nights, and completed them with ten [more]; so the appointment of his Lord was completed in forty nights.* (7:142). The appointment was mentioned as forty nights here either by way of predominance (التغليب) or because the final ten nights were a separate appointment. Thus, the forty is the sum of the two appointments, as mentioned in the narrations.

**His saying, the Exalted:** *so turn in repentance to your Creator* (فتوبوا إلى بارئكم). The Creator (الباريء) is one of the Most Beautiful Names (الأسماء الحسنى), as the Exalted said: **هُوَ اللَّهُ الْخَالِقُ الْبَارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ لَهُ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ** — *He is Allah, the Creator, the Maker, the Formed; to Him belong the Best Names.* (59:24). It occurs in three places in His speech: two of them are in this verse. Perhaps this Name was specifically chosen here among other suitable names because its meaning is close to "the Creator" and "the Originator," derived from *bara’a* (برء), which means to separate (فصل), because He separates creation from non-existence or man from the earth. It is as if the Exalted is saying: "This repentance and your killing of yourselves—though it be the most difficult of commands—is commanded by Allah, who brought you into being (برئكم). He who loved your existence, which is good for you, now loves for death to befall you [as a purification], so it is good for you. How could He not love what is good for you when He is the one who created you (برئكم)?" Thus, the choice of the term "the Creator" (الباريء) and its annexation to them in His saying "to your Creator" (إلى بارئكم) and "with your Creator" (عند بارئكم) is to signal a special relationship (الاختصاص) to stir feelings of love.

**His saying, the Exalted:** *That is better for you with your Creator* (ذلكم خير لكم عند بارئكم). The apparent sense of the verse and what preceded it is that these addresses—and the enumeration of their various transgressions and sins—are attributed to the whole group despite being committed by only some of them. This is because they were a unified community with a single national identity (قومية), where some were pleased with the actions of others. Thus, the actions of some are attributed to the rest due to the unity (الوحدة) existing among them. Not all of the Children of Israel worshipped the calf, nor did all of them kill the prophets, among other sins. Based on this, His saying, the Exalted, *and slay yourselves* (واقتلوا أنفسكم) means the killing of some of them—namely those who worshipped the calf—as indicated by His saying: *Indeed you have wronged yourselves by taking up the calf* (إنكم ظلمتم أنفسكم باتخاذكم العجل). His saying, *That is better for you with your Creator* (ذلكم خير لكم عند بارئكم) is the completion of the account of Moses' speech, as is apparent. His saying, *so He turned to you clemently* (فتاب عليكم) indicates the descent of repentance and its acceptance. Narrations state that repentance was granted before all the criminals among them were killed.

From this, it becomes clear that the command was a trial (إمتحاني), similar to what occurred in the story of Abraham's (عليه السلام) vision and the sacrifice of Ishmael: **يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ الرُّؤْيَا** — *‘O Abraham! You have indeed fulfilled the vision!’* (37:105). Moses (عليه السلام) mentioned: *so turn in repentance to your Creator and slay yourselves; that is better for you with your Creator*, and Allah, the Glorified, ratified his (عليه السلام) statement, considering the killing of some as the killing of all, and sent down repentance by His saying: *so He turned to you clemently* (فتاب عليكم).

**His saying, the Exalted:** *a plague from the sky* (رجزاً من السماء). *Rijz* (الرجز) is punishment (العذاب).

**His saying, the Exalted:** *and do not act wickedly* (ولا تعثوا). *‘Ayth* (العيث) and *‘atha* (العثى) refer to the most severe form of corruption (الفساد).

**His saying, the Exalted:** *its cucumbers, its garlic* (وقثائها وفومها). *Qiththa’* (القثاء) is cucumber (الخيار) and *fum* (الفوم) is garlic (الثوم) or wheat (الحنطة).

**His saying, the Exalted:** *and they became deserving of the wrath* (وبائوا بغضب), meaning they returned (رجعوا).

**His saying, the Exalted:** *That was because they used to deny* (ذلك بأنهم كانوا يكفرون) is the causal explanation (تعليل) for what preceded it.

**His saying, the Exalted:** *That was because they were rebellious* (ذلك بما عصوا) is a causal explanation for the previous explanation. Thus, their rebellion and their persistence in transgression (الاعتداء) were the causes of their disbelief in the signs and their killing of the prophets, as the Exalted said: **ثُمَّ كَانَ عَاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا السُّوأَىٰ أَن كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَكَانُوا بِهَا يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ** — *Then the fate of those who committed misdeeds was the worst [fate], for they denied the signs of Allah and used to deride them.* (30:10). Regarding the causal explanation through rebellion (المعصية), there is a perspective that will be discussed in the upcoming section.

## (Narrative Discourse)

In *Tafsir al-Ayyashi*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: "And when We appointed for Moses forty nights," it is narrated from Abu Ja‘far (عليه السلام) that he said: "It was in the [divine] knowledge and decree thirty nights, then a change in the creative decree (بدا) occurred and He added ten; thus the first and the last appointment of his Lord was completed as forty nights."

**I say:** This narration supports what was mentioned earlier—that the forty is the sum of two appointments.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, narrated from ‘Ali (عليه السلام) regarding His saying, the Exalted: *And when Moses said to his people, ‘O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves...’* the verse. He said: "They said to Moses: 'What is our repentance?' He said: 'Some of you shall kill others.' So they took knives, and a man would kill his brother, his father, and his son, and by Allah, he did not care whom he killed, until seventy thousand of them were killed. Then Allah revealed to Moses: 'Command them to stay their hands; He has forgiven those who were killed and accepted the repentance of those who remain.'"

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*: He (عليه السلام) said: "When Moses went out to the appointed time and returned to his people while they had worshipped the calf, Moses said to them: 'O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves by taking up the calf, so turn in repentance to your Creator and slay yourselves; that is better for you with your Creator.' They said to him: 'How shall we kill ourselves?' Moses said to them: 'Go each one of you to the Holy House (بيت المقدس) with a knife, a piece of iron, or a sword. When I ascend the pulpit of the Children of Israel, you must be masked so that no one recognizes his companion, and kill one another.' So seventy thousand men from those who had worshipped the calf gathered at the Holy House. When Moses had prayed with them and ascended the pulpit, they began killing one another until Gabriel descended and said: 'Tell them, O Moses, to stop the killing, for Allah has accepted their repentance.' Ten thousand of them had been killed, and Allah sent down: *that is better for you with your Creator, so He turned to you clemently; indeed He is the All-clement, the All-merciful.*"

**I say:** This narration, as you see, indicates that His saying, the Exalted, *That is better for you with your Creator* was spoken by Moses and also spoken by Him, the Glorified. Thus, it was a ratification of a word spoken by Moses and a disclosure that it was complete, contrary to what might appear from the literal sense that it was incomplete. For the literal sense suggests that Moses made the killing of everyone a "good" for them with their Creator, yet only some were killed rather than all. Thus, the Glorified made the killing that actually occurred the "good" that Moses (عليه السلام) had mentioned, as previously explained.

Also in *Tafsir al-Qummi*: regarding His saying, the Exalted: *And We shaded you with clouds...* the verse. It is narrated that when Moses crossed the sea with the Children of Israel, they descended into a wilderness (مفازة). They said: "O Moses, you have destroyed us, killed us, and brought us out from inhabited land to a wilderness where there is no shade, no trees, and no water." So a cloud would come during the day to shade them from the sun, and manna (المنّ) would descend upon them at night, falling on the plants, trees, and stones for them to eat. In the evening, a roasted bird would come to them and land on their tables; when they had eaten and drunk, it would fly away. Moses had a stone with him which he would place in the middle of the camp and strike with his staff, and twelve springs would gush forth from it, as Allah narrated, going to each tribe in its quarters—and they were twelve tribes.

In *al-Kafi*: regarding His saying, the Exalted: *They did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves*, narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Madi (عليه السلام) who said: "Indeed, Allah is too Mighty and too Protected to be wronged or to attribute wrong to Himself. However, He has joined us with Himself (خلطنا بنفسه), making the wrong done to us as wrong done to Him, and our guardianship (ولايتنا) as His guardianship. Then Allah sent down the Quran regarding this to His Prophet, saying: *They did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves.*" The narrator said: "I asked: 'Is this a revelation (تنزيل)?'" He replied: "Yes."

**I say:** A similar narration is also reported from al-Baqir (عليه السلام). His (عليه السلام) saying "too Protected to be wronged" (أمنع من أن يظلم)—in the passive voice—is an explanation of His saying, the Exalted: *They did not wrong Us*. His saying "or to attribute wrong to Himself" (أو ينسب نفسه إلى الظلم)—in the active voice—and his saying "but He joined us with Himself" means He joined us—the assembly of Prophets, Successors, and Imams—with Himself. His saying "I asked: 'Is this a revelation?' He replied: 'Yes'" is explained by the fact that negation in such instances is only valid where affirmation is possible or where its possibility might be imagined. One does not say of a wall that it "does not see" or "does not wrong" except for a specific rhetorical point (نكتة). He, the Glorified, is too exalted for His speech to allow the delusion that He could be wronged or that it could occur to Him. Thus, the rhetorical point in this negation is the aforementioned "joining," because great figures often speak on behalf of their servants and assistants.

In *Tafsir al-Ayyashi*: regarding His saying, the Exalted: *That was because they used to deny the signs of Allah...* the verse. Narrated from al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) that he read this verse: *That was because they used to deny the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly; that was because they were rebellious and used to transgress.* He said: "By Allah, they did not strike them with their hands nor kill them with their swords, but they heard their secrets and broadcasted them (أذاعوها), so they were seized because of that and killed. Thus, it was killing, transgression, and a calamity."

**I say:** A similar narration is in *al-Kafi* from him (عليه السلام). It is as if he (عليه السلام) derived this from His saying, the Exalted: *that was because they were rebellious and used to transgress*, for killing—especially the killing of prophets—and disbelief in the signs of Allah are not typically explained by mere "rebellion" (عصيان); rather, the matter is the reverse due to the severity and gravity of those acts. However, when rebellion is understood as the failure to maintain secrecy and caution, such a causal explanation becomes valid.
